## Preliminaries: Idealistic Behavior {.nav-feedback}

How can we model idealistic behavior?

Agents optimize *cummulative return*, or value $V$, by adhering to optimal behavior $\pi^*$
\begin{align}
V_{\pi^*}(s_0) = \sum_t R(s_t,a_t), \qquad s_{t+1} \sim T(s_t, a_t) ,~a_{t} \sim \pi^*(s_t)
\end{align}

We can model this via **Boltzmann Rationality**.

::: {.callout-tip title="Definition: Boltzmann Rationality"}
A perfectly rational agent can be modeled via the recursive relationship found in Boltzmann Rationality,
\begin{align}
P(a \mid s) \propto \exp(\alpha Q(s,a)), \qquad Q(s, a) = R(s, a) + \mathbb{E}[Q(s',a')]
\end{align}
where $s' \sim T(\cdot \mid s,a),~a' \sim P(\cdot \mid s')$ and $Q$ is the quality function.
:::
